title: Stop extracting data: Run serverless Python natively in BigQuery!
summary: Google Cloud announced general availability of Python User Defined Functions in BigQuery, enabling users to run custom Python code natively within SQL statements without infrastructure management. The feature supports importing libraries like BeautifulSoup and running machine learning tokenizers directly in queries.
writeup: The video covers the general availability of Python User Defined Functions in BigQuery, which let custom Python code run natively inside SQL statements without provisioning or managing infrastructure. This matters because it removes the need to extract data out of BigQuery for Python processing, keeping computation alongside the data. The concrete takeaways: UDFs run serverless, support importing libraries such as BeautifulSoup, and can execute machine learning tokenizers directly within queries.
